rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 3, 2024, Line 11. PETITIONER SHELBY BATES' MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION. Petitioner Bates' petition/motion to compel arbitration and stay is granted. The court orders the parties to arbitrate Bates' dispute. There is no cited authority holding that the court can order arbitration as to the other claimants or order defendant to pay a withdrawn invoice that also pertains to the other claimants. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties.) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: BUTCHERSHOP CREATIVE, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. CFO RICK INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 3, 2024, Line 5. 1 - CROSS DEFENDANT TREVOR HUBBARD's DEMURRER to 1ST Amended CROSS COMPLAINT. Trevor Hubbard's demurrer to the first amended cross-complaint (FACC) is overruled. Count 2 adequately pleads a claim for breach of oral contracts - the at-issue loans. (FACC 13:1-16.) Whether Hubbard's personal guarantees were oral is immaterial because the loans were pled to be oral: a "general demurrer does not lie to a portion of a cause of action and, if any part of a cause of action is properly pleaded, the demurrer will be overruled." (Elder v. Pacific Bell Telephone Co, (2012) 205 Cal.App.4th 841, 856 n.14.) Further, the FACC adequately pleads Hubbard and Butchershop Creative, LLC were alter egos. (FAC 3:10-23, 11:5-12:8.) Count 3 adequately pleads fraud with specificity. (Opp. 12:27-14:5.) Justifiable reliance is question of fact not properly decided on demurrer. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: BUTCHERSHOP CREATIVE, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. CFO RICK INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 3, 2024, Line 5. 2 - CROSS DEFENDANT BUTCHERSHOP CREATIVE, LLC's DEMURRER to 1ST Amended CROSS COMPLAINT. Butchershop Creative, LLC's demurrer to the first amended cross-complaint (FACC) is overruled. Count 2 adequately pleads a claim for a breach of oral contracts - the at-issue loans. (FACC 13:1-16.) Whether Trevor Hubbard's personal guarantees were oral is immaterial because the loans were oral: a "demurrer does not lie to a portion of a cause of action and, if any part of a cause of action is properly pleaded, the demurrer will be overruled." (Elder v. Pacific Bell Telephone Co, (2012) 205 Cal.App.4th 841, 856 n.14.) Further, the FACC adequately pleads Hubbard and Butchershop were alter egos. (FAC 3:10-23, 11:5-12:8.) Count 3 is pled only against Hubbard (FACC 13:18), so Butchershop lacks standing to demur to it. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. rulings.Case Title: STUDENT LOAN SOLUTIONS, LLC VS. CURTIS U COOPER ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 3, 2024, Line 6. CROSS DEFENDANT STUDENT LOAN SOLUTIONS, LLC, CHRISTOPHER RUH, AND GOLDSMITH & HULL, APC's DEMURRER to 2ND Amended CROSS COMPLAINT. Student Loan Solutions, Inc., Christopher Ruh and Goldsmith & Hull, APC's (SLS defendants) demurrer to Curtis Cooper's second amended cross-complaint (SACC) is overruled. Earlier this year, SLS sued Cooper over student loan debt. The SACC pleads that the SLS complaint violates 2022's Private Student Loan Collections Reform Act (PSLCRA: Civ. Code 1788.200 et seq.) and two other debt collection acts in ways "misleading, deceptive and abusive" to Cooper. (Id. at 14:9-15, 10:7-13:25.) The PSLCRA is designed to protect "student loan borrowers" such as Cooper. (Civ. Code 1788.201(a).) A private right of action and statutory damages are provided (id. at 1788.208(a)) and Cooper pleads injury. He has standing to sue. The SLS defendants say they "complied with" debt collection laws (Memo. 15:21), but concede compliance was less than complete. (Id. at 16:1, 18:12, 19:11.) Moreover, a "demurrer is simply not the appropriate procedure for determining the truth of disputed facts." (Ramsden v. Western Union (1977) 71 Cal.App.3d 873, 879.) And the "hearing on demurrer may not be turned into a contested evidentiary hearing through the guise of having the court take judicial notice" (Del Webb Corp. v. Structural Materials Co. (1981) 123 Cal.App.3d 593, 605.) Further, several opinions defendants rely on are out-of-jurisdiction and do not apply our courts' liberal pleading standards. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: CALASIA CONSTRUCTION, INC., A CALIFORNIA VS. CHOTTO MATTE (SAN FRANCISCO) LLC, A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 3, 2024, Line 13. DEFENDANT CHOTTO MATTE (SAN FRANCISCO) LLC's Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action. Chotto Matte's motion to compel arbitration and stay is granted. The general and subcontracts among Pribuss, CalAsia, and Chotto Matte broadly call for arbitration of this dispute. CalAsia and Pribuss argue that the court should not compel arbitration because landlord PPF, who is named on their mechanic's lien claims, is not obliged to arbitrate and there is a risk of inconsistent rulings. (CCP 1281.2(c).) CCP 1281.2(d) gives the court broad discretion where there is risk of inconsistent rulings. Here, the court exercises its discretion to compel arbitration and stay the remainder of this action, e.g., the foreclosure on mechanic's lien claim. This ruling is consistent with CCP 1281.5, which contemplates that a contractor's mechanic's lien claim will be held in abeyance until the arbitration is complete. (Von Becelaere Ventures, LLC v. Zenovic (2018) 24 Cal.App.5th 243, 249.) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)
